header.name: cache-control
header.info:
Postman added "Cache-Control: no-cache" as a precautionary measure to prevent the server from returning stale response when one makes repeated requests.

You can remove this header in the app settings or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: postman-token
header.info:
The Postman-Token header appends a random UUID to every outgoing request. Postman adds this header for API developers to better debug requests sent and to ensure separate requests appear distinct to the receiving server.

You can remove this header in the app settings.

header.name: content-type
header.info:
The Content-Type header is added to help the server identify the media type of the request body that is present in this request.

Use the request body tab to control the value or to remove this header.

header.name: content-length
header.info:
The Content-Length header was added to indicate to the server the size of the request body that is added to this request. Server uses this value to parse the request body accurately.

You can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: host
header.info:
The Host header is added to identify the domain name for which a request is being sent to the server. This header is implicitly sent by every HTTP client.

You can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value. It is most likely that without this header, your request will return an HTTP 400 error.

header.name: user-agent
header.info:
The User-Agent header is added to help the server identify Postman as the HTTP requesting application or client.

It is recommended that this header be sent, but you can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: accept
header.info:
The "Accept: */*" header is added to tell the server that Postman can understand and process all forms of response content types.

It is recommended that this header be sent, but you can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value.

header.name: accept-encoding
header.info:
The Accept-Encoding header is added to indicate to the server that Postman HTTP client supports a defined list of content-encoding or compression algorithms as response.

You can remove the header or enter a new one with a different value. Doing that may not accurately render the response within the app.

header.name: connection
header.info:
Postman added the Connection header to indicate the server to keep the underlying network connection open once the current response is received. This allows Postman to reuse the same connection for faster response times in subsequent requests to the same server.

You can remove this header or enter a new one with a different value, such as `Connection: Close` to control this behaviour.

request.description:
Upserts step 1 data for the authenticated user's enrollment.

Preconditions
- Requires Bearer `{{accessToken}}`.
- `POST /enrollment/start` must already have created the enrollment.
- Consent must already be accepted or `ConsentAcceptedGuard` returns `403 Consent not accepted`.
- Enrollment must still be in `DRAFT` status or the service returns `400 Enrollment is not in draft status`.
- Global validation uses `whitelist: true` and `forbidNonWhitelisted: true`, so unknown fields are rejected.

Canonical enum-style values
- `gender.gender`: `MALE`, `FEMALE`, `NON_BINARY`, `TWO_SPIRIT`, `SELF_DESCRIBE`, `PREFER_NOT_TO_SAY`, `OTHER`
- `contact.phoneType`: `MOBILE`, `HOME`, `WORK`
- `additionalInfo.maritalStatus`: `SINGLE`, `MARRIED`, `DIVORCED`, `WIDOWED`
- The service maps these values case-insensitively, but frontend should send the canonical uppercase values above.

Field contract
- `legalName`: required object
- `legalName.firstName`: string, required, stored in `Enrollment.firstName`
- `legalName.middleName`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.middleName`
- `legalName.lastName`: string, required, stored in `Enrollment.lastName`
- `legalName.maternalLastName`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.maternalLastName`
- `legalName.preferredName`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.preferredName`
- `birthInfo`: required object
- `birthInfo.dateOfBirth`: valid date string, required, transformed to `Date` and stored in `Enrollment.dateOfBirth`; ISO-8601 is safest
- `birthInfo.cityOfBirth`: string, required, stored in `Enrollment.cityOfBirth`
- `birthInfo.municipalityOfBirth`: string, required, stored in `Enrollment.municipalityOfBirth`
- `birthInfo.countryOfBirth`: string, required, stored in `Enrollment.countryOfBirth`
- `gender`: required object
- `gender.gender`: string, required, must match the allowed values above, stored in `Enrollment.gender`
- `gender.pronouns`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.pronouns`
- `contact`: required object
- `contact.email`: valid email string, required, stored in `Contact.email` and does not update the auth user's `User.email`
- `contact.phoneNumber`: string, required, stored in `Contact.phoneNumber`
- `contact.phoneType`: string, required, must match the allowed values above, stored in `Contact.phoneType`
- `contact.allowSMS`: boolean, optional, stored in `Contact.allowSMS`; omitted on create uses DB default `false`, omitted on update leaves the previous value unchanged
- `currentAddress`: required object; backend automatically stores it as `Address.type = CURRENT`
- `currentAddress.street`: string, required, stored in `Address.street`
- `currentAddress.city`: string, required, stored in `Address.city`
- `currentAddress.state`: string, required, stored in `Address.state`
- `currentAddress.zipCode`: string, required, stored in `Address.zipCode`
- `currentAddress.country`: string, required, stored in `Address.country`
- `mailingAddress`: required object; backend automatically stores it as `Address.type = MAILING`
- `mailingAddress.street`: string, required, stored in `Address.street`
- `mailingAddress.city`: string, required, stored in `Address.city`
- `mailingAddress.state`: string, required, stored in `Address.state`
- `mailingAddress.zipCode`: string, required, stored in `Address.zipCode`
- `mailingAddress.country`: string, required, stored in `Address.country`
- `emergencyContact`: required object
- `emergencyContact.fullName`: string, required, stored in `EmergencyContact.fullName`
- `emergencyContact.relationship`: string, required, stored in `EmergencyContact.relationship`
- `emergencyContact.phoneNumber`: string, required, stored in `EmergencyContact.phoneNumber`
- `additionalInfo`: required object
- `additionalInfo.maritalStatus`: string, optional, allowed values listed above, stored in `Enrollment.maritalStatus`; defaults to `SINGLE` when omitted
- `additionalInfo.occupation`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.occupation`
- `additionalInfo.educationLevel`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.educationLevel`
- `additionalInfo.languagesSpoken`: array of strings, optional, stored in `Enrollment.languagesSpoken`
- `additionalInfo.specialSkills`: string, optional, stored in `Enrollment.specialSkills`

Important implementation notes from current backend code
- `currentAddress.apartment`, `currentAddress.yearsLived`, `mailingAddress.apartment`, and `mailingAddress.yearsLived` are accepted by the DTO but are currently not persisted by `Step1Service`.
- Do not send address `type`; the backend derives `CURRENT` and `MAILING` internally.
- On success the API returns `{ "success": true }` and marks enrollment step 1 as completed.

Upserts step 2 maternal lineage data for the authenticated user's enrollment.

Preconditions
- Requires Bearer `{{accessToken}}`.
- `POST /enrollment/start` must already have created the enrollment.
- Consent must already be accepted or `ConsentAcceptedGuard` returns `403 Consent not accepted`.
- Enrollment must still be in `DRAFT` status or the service returns `400 Enrollment is not in draft status`.
- Global validation uses `whitelist: true` and `forbidNonWhitelisted: true`, so unknown fields are rejected.

Allowed enum values
- `relation`: `MOTHER`, `GRANDMOTHER`, `GREAT_GRANDMOTHER`, `GREAT_GREAT_GRANDMOTHER`, `GREAT_GREAT_GREAT_GRANDMOTHER`
- `livingStatus`: `LIVING`, `DECEASED`

Payload contract
- `maternalLineages`: required array
- `maternalLineages[].id`: optional string; omit for new records, include the existing `MaternalLineage.id` when updating an existing row
- `maternalLineages[].relation`: required enum string; stored in `MaternalLineage.relation`
- `maternalLineages[].fullName`: required non-empty string; stored in `MaternalLineage.fullName`
- `maternalLineages[].maidenName`: optional string; stored in `MaternalLineage.maidenName`
- `maternalLineages[].dateOfBirth`: optional valid date string; transformed to `Date` and stored in `MaternalLineage.dateOfBirth`; ISO-8601 is safest
- `maternalLineages[].placeOfBirth`: optional string; stored in `MaternalLineage.placeOfBirth`
- `maternalLineages[].livingStatus`: required enum string; stored in `MaternalLineage.livingStatus`
- `maternalLineages[].approximateBirthYear`: optional integer number, must be `>= 1800`; stored in `MaternalLineage.approximateBirthYear`
- `maternalLineages[].regionOfOrigin`: optional string; stored in `MaternalLineage.regionOfOrigin`
- `maternalLineages[].familyOccupation`: optional string; stored in `MaternalLineage.familyOccupation`
- `maternalLineages[].additionalNotes`: optional string; stored in `MaternalLineage.additionalNotes`

Important behavior
- This endpoint syncs the full `maternalLineages` array. It is not a partial patch.
- Existing maternal lineage rows for the current enrollment that are not included by `id` in the incoming array are deleted.
- New rows are created when `id` is omitted.
- Existing rows are updated when `id` is included.
- Existing lineage ids can be read from `GET /account/info` under `enrollment.maternalLineages`.
- Per current backend code, an incoming `id` is updated by primary key only, so frontend should only send ids that belong to the authenticated user's own enrollment.
- `maternalLineages` may be an empty array under current validation; sending an empty array clears all existing maternal lineage rows and still marks step 2 complete.
- On success the API returns `{ "success": true }` and marks enrollment step 2 as completed.

body.raw:
{
  "maternalLineages": [
    {
      "relation": "MOTHER",
      "fullName": "Anita Sahoo",
      "maidenName": "Das",
      "dateOfBirth": "1975-06-15T00:00:00.000Z",
      "placeOfBirth": "Kolkata",
      "livingStatus": "LIVING",
      "approximateBirthYear": 1975,
      "regionOfOrigin": "West Bengal",
      "familyOccupation": "Teacher",
      "additionalNotes": "Loves gardening"
    },
    {
      "relation": "GRANDMOTHER",
      "fullName": "Saraswati Das",
      "maidenName": "Mukherjee",
      "dateOfBirth": "1950-03-10T00:00:00.000Z",
      "placeOfBirth": "Bardhaman",
      "livingStatus": "DECEASED",
      "approximateBirthYear": 1950,
      "regionOfOrigin": "West Bengal",
      "familyOccupation": "Homemaker",
      "additionalNotes": "Known for traditional cooking"
    },
    {
      "relation": "GREAT_GRANDMOTHER",
      "fullName": "Kamala Mukherjee",
      "maidenName": "Banerjee",
      "livingStatus": "DECEASED",
      "approximateBirthYear": 1925,
      "regionOfOrigin": "Bengal Presidency",
      "familyOccupation": "Agriculture",
      "additionalNotes": "Lived during pre-independence era"
    },
    {
      "relation": "GREAT_GREAT_GRANDMOTHER",
      "fullName": "Charulata Banerjee",
      "livingStatus": "DECEASED",
      "approximateBirthYear": 1900,
      "regionOfOrigin": "Undivided Bengal",
      "familyOccupation": "Handloom weaving",
      "additionalNotes": "Part of a large joint family"
    },
    {
      "relation": "GREAT_GREAT_GREAT_GRANDMOTHER",
      "fullName": "Unknown Devi",
      "livingStatus": "DECEASED",
      "approximateBirthYear": 1875,
      "regionOfOrigin": "Rural Bengal",
      "familyOccupation": "Farming",
      "additionalNotes": "Limited historical records available"
    }
  ]
}

request.description:
Deletes one maternal lineage entry for the authenticated user's enrollment.

Preconditions
- Requires Bearer `{{accessToken}}`.
- `POST /enrollment/start` must already have created the enrollment.
- Consent must already be accepted or `ConsentAcceptedGuard` returns `403 Consent not accepted`.
- Enrollment must still be in `DRAFT` status or the service returns `400 Enrollment is not in draft status`.

Path param contract
- `:id`: required maternal lineage id string.
- Use an id from `GET /account/info` -> `enrollment.maternalLineages[].id`.
- The backend deletes only when both the supplied `id` and the current enrollment match.

Important behavior
- The service uses `deleteMany`, so the API still returns `{ "success": true }` even when the id does not exist or does not belong to the current enrollment.
- This endpoint does not change step completion state.
